findings.title: Two incompatible header/metadata formats across modules
findings.description:
Modules use two different header formats. Format A (M01, M02, M03, M09, M10, M11, M12) uses an H2 subtitle line: '## Agentic AI Engineering — 3-Day Intensive Course'. Format B (M04, M05, M06, M07, M08) uses bold metadata fields: '**Course**: Agentic AI Engineering — 3-Day Intensive
**Module**: 06 of 12
**Duration**: 90 minutes
**Day**: 2, Morning'. Format B includes module number, duration, and day placement; Format A omits all three. This split falls roughly along the rewrite boundary (M01-M03 were expanded later; M04-M08 were written in a different pass).
findings.recommendation: Adopt Format B (bold metadata) for all modules. It provides more useful information (duration, day, module number). Apply it to M01, M02, M03, M09, M10, M11, M12.

findings.title: Learning Objectives section format inconsistency
findings.description: Two formats exist. Format A (M01, M02, M03, M09, M10, M11, M12) embeds objectives inline within the Overview as '**Learning Objectives:**' (bold text, no separate heading). Format B (M04, M05, M06, M07, M08) uses a dedicated '## Learning Objectives' H2 section separated from Overview by a horizontal rule. Format B is cleaner for navigation and scanability.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on Format B: a standalone '## Learning Objectives' section after the Overview, separated by a horizontal rule.

findings.title: Section numbering uses inconsistent heading levels
findings.description: Content section headings vary between ## (H2) and ### (H3). M01 uses '## 1.1', '## 1.2' etc. M02 uses '## 2.1', '## 2.2'. M03 uses '## 3.1', '## 3.2'. M09-M12 use '## 9.1', '## 10.1' etc. But M04-M08 use '### 4.1', '### 5.1', '### 6.1', '### 7.1', '### 8.1'. This means content sections in M04-M08 are one heading level deeper than in other modules, which affects navigation, table of contents generation, and visual hierarchy.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on ## (H2) for all numbered content sections (X.1, X.2, etc.), with ### (H3) for subsections within them. This matches the majority pattern (M01-M03, M09-M12).

findings.title: Key Concepts / Summary section missing from 9 of 12 modules
findings.description: Only M01, M02, and M03 have a '## Key Concepts for Review' section at the end, providing a glossary-style summary of key terms. Modules M04 through M12 lack any equivalent summary or glossary section. This is a significant structural gap — the first three modules set an expectation for end-of-module review content that the remaining nine do not fulfill.
findings.recommendation: Add a '## Key Concepts for Review' section to all modules M04-M12, matching the format used in M01-M03.

findings.title: Do/Don't section missing from 6 of 12 modules
findings.description: Only M09, M10, M11, and M12 have a formal '## ✅ Do / ❌ Don't' section. M01 has a subsection '### Do / Don't' within a broader section. M04, M05, M06, M07, and M08 use scattered inline ✅/❌ markers but no dedicated section. M02 and M03 have no Do/Don't content at all. Modules M04-M08 interleave Do/Don't guidance within content sections rather than consolidating it.
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated '## Do / Don't' section to every module, placed consistently before Further Reading. Consolidate scattered inline ✅/❌ guidance into this section where appropriate.

findings.title: Companion Media section present in only 5 of 12 modules
findings.description: M01, M02, M03, M07, and M08 include a '## Companion Media (NotebookLM)' section with links to slide decks and explainer videos. Modules M04, M05, M06, M09, M10, M11, and M12 lack this section entirely. If companion media exists for all modules, the section should be present in all. If it only exists for some, the inconsistency should be documented.
findings.recommendation: Either add Companion Media sections to all 12 modules (generating media as needed) or remove them from M01-M03, M07-M08 and link them from a central resource page instead.

findings.title: Navigation link format varies between modules
findings.description: M01 and M02 use descriptive navigation links: '[Next Module → Module 02: Claude Code Foundations](/module/02)'. M03 through M12 use shorter format: '[Next Module → Module 04](/module/04)'. M01 also lacks a Previous Module link (acceptable as first module). The descriptive format in M01-M02 is more helpful for orientation.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on the descriptive format: '[Next Module → Module XX: Title](/module/XX)' for all modules.

findings.title: PRAO consistently capitalized but expansion varies
findings.description: PRAO is consistently used as an uppercase acronym across all modules. The expansion 'Perceive, Reason, Act, Observe' is always written with initial capitals when spelled out. The expanded form 'Perceive → Reason → Act → Observe' with arrows is used in M01, M04, M05. 'PRAO cycle' vs 'PRAO loop' usage varies — M01 introduces it as 'PRAO loop' but M04 and M05 call it 'PRAO cycle'. Both appear in M12.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on 'PRAO loop' as the primary term (matching the M01 introduction) and note 'PRAO cycle' as an acceptable synonym once per module at most.

findings.title: TCEF consistently used; GCCF confined to M04 only
findings.description: TCEF (Task + Context + Examples + Format) is used consistently across M04, M07, M08, M11, and M12. The expansion is always 'Task + Context + Examples + Format' or 'Task, Context, Examples, and Format'. GCCF (Goal + Context + Constraints + Format) appears only in M04, where it is introduced and contrasted with TCEF. This is appropriate — GCCF is presented as the simpler pattern that TCEF supersedes.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. TCEF and GCCF terminology is handled correctly.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md capitalization is consistent
findings.description: All 135 occurrences of 'CLAUDE.md' across 8 modules use the correct all-caps 'CLAUDE.md' form. No instances of 'claude.md' or 'Claude.md' were found. This is correct and consistent.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: MCP first-use expansion missing in most modules
findings.description: The full expansion 'Model Context Protocol (MCP)' appears in M02 and M05. Other modules that use MCP heavily — M06, M09, M10, M11, M12 — use only the abbreviation without expansion on first use within that module. Best practice for courseware: expand on first use in each module since modules may be read independently.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Model Context Protocol (MCP)' expansion on first use in each module, or at minimum in M06 (which is the second MCP module and should not assume M05 was just read).

findings.title: Streamable HTTP terminology is consistent
findings.description: The term 'Streamable HTTP' (capitalized S) is used consistently across M02, M05, M11. SSE deprecation is correctly noted. No instances of 'streamable HTTP' (lowercase s) or 'HTTP streaming' were found. The course correctly and consistently uses the MCP specification's terminology.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: Extended thinking capitalization varies
findings.description: M03 consistently uses lowercase 'extended thinking' throughout both prose and headings (e.g., '## 3.5 Extended Thinking' for the heading but 'extended thinking is a model-level capability' in body text). The heading uses title case while the body uses lowercase. This is actually correct English convention (title case in headings, lowercase in running text). No instances of 'Extended Thinking' appear mid-sentence as a proper noun, which is correct.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. The capitalization follows standard English conventions.

findings.title: Instructor voice is consistent second-person throughout
findings.description: All 12 modules consistently use second-person address ('you will be able to', 'you need to', 'your role'). The instructor voice is authoritative and direct throughout. There are no shifts to first person ('I recommend') or third person ('the student should'). The formality level is consistent: professional but not academic, direct but not informal. The prose reads as if written by a single author with a unified editorial voice.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Voice consistency is strong.

findings.title: Modules M01-M03 have a richer, more discursive tone than M04-M08
findings.description: M01 (7,317 words), M02 (7,502 words), and M03 (8,526 words) are substantially longer and more discursive than M04-M08 (3,100-3,500 words each). The early modules include more motivational framing, more worked examples, more anticipation of student questions, and more transitions between sections. M04-M08 are more compact and reference-like — they present concepts efficiently but with less narrative connective tissue. This is partly explained by the interactive HTML diagram elements in M01-M03 that M04-M08 lack, but even accounting for markup, the prose density differs.
findings.recommendation: This is not necessarily a defect — later modules may benefit from being more concise as students are more oriented. However, if the goal is uniform depth, M04-M08 could benefit from additional worked examples, motivational context, and transitional prose to match the richness of M01-M03.

findings.title: All code blocks have language identifiers
findings.description: Every fenced code block across all 12 modules includes a language identifier (bash, json, typescript, markdown, yaml, python, go, html, mermaid). No bare ``` opening fences were found that were not closing fences. This is excellent consistency.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: Interactive HTML diagrams present in M01-M03 but absent from M04-M12
findings.description: Modules M01, M02, and M03 contain extensive interactive HTML diagram markup using custom components: 'ix-diagram', 'data-component="click-cards"', 'data-component="tabbed-panel"', 'data-component="step-walkthrough"', 'data-component="predict-reveal"', 'data-component="quiz"'. These provide rich interactive learning experiences. Modules M04-M08 use only static Mermaid diagrams. M09-M12 also use Mermaid. The absence of interactive elements in M04-M12 represents a significant experiential inconsistency.
findings.recommendation: This is likely an in-progress enhancement. Prioritize adding interactive diagrams to M04-M12 to match the M01-M03 experience, or document this as a known gap with a timeline.

findings.title: Lab number to module mapping has gaps and shared labs
findings.description: Lab-to-module mapping: M01→Lab 01, M02→Lab 02, M03→Lab 03, M04→Lab 05, M05→Lab 04, M06→Lab 04, M07→Lab 06, M08→Lab 06, M09→Lab 07, M10→Lab 10, M11→Lab 11, M12→Lab 12. Issues: (1) M05 and M06 both reference Lab 04 — is this one lab covering both modules? (2) M07 and M08 both reference Lab 06. (3) M04 references Lab 05 but M05 references Lab 04, which is a confusing inversion — Lab 04 covers MCP (Module 05-06) while Lab 05 covers prompting (Module 04). (4) No lab is referenced for Labs 08 or 09, leaving a gap in the sequence.
findings.recommendation: Review and rationalize the lab numbering so that Lab N corresponds to Module N (or close to it). The current inversion where Module 04 maps to Lab 05 and Module 05 maps to Lab 04 is confusing. Consider whether shared labs (Lab 04 for M05+M06, Lab 06 for M07+M08) should be documented more clearly.

findings.title: Forward references in M01 are fulfilled
findings.description: M01 makes two forward references: 'CLAUDE.md files (global and project-level, covered in Module 02)' and 'In Module 02, you'll build a full CLAUDE.md'. Both are fulfilled by Module 02, Section 2.2. M03 references 'the approval pattern from Module 02' which corresponds to permissions content in M02. M06 references 'Module 05 established the architecture of MCP' which is accurate. M11 references 'The TCEF prompt structure...introduced in Module 04' — accurate. M12 references 'the decision framework from Module 09' and 'the security framework from Module 10' — both accurate.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Cross-references are accurate.

findings.title: Significant word count disparity between module groups
findings.description: Word counts reveal three tiers: Tier 1 (7,300-8,500 words): M01 (7,317), M02 (7,502), M03 (8,526). Tier 2 (3,900-5,000 words): M09 (4,301), M10 (4,552), M11 (3,951), M12 (4,969). Tier 3 (3,100-3,500 words): M04 (3,327), M05 (3,168), M06 (3,157), M07 (3,487), M08 (3,413). The Tier 1 modules are 2-2.7x longer than Tier 3 modules. Much of this is interactive HTML markup in M01-M03, but even accounting for that, the prose content in M01-M03 is substantially richer. M04-M08 cover topics of comparable importance (prompt engineering, MCP, skills, meta-prompting) at roughly half the depth.
findings.recommendation: Expand M04-M08 to approximately 5,000-6,000 words each. Priority additions: worked examples, transitional prose, interactive diagrams (matching M01-M03 format), and Key Concepts for Review sections.

findings.title: Research papers section appears only in M04
findings.description: Module 04 includes a '### Research Papers' subsection under Further Reading with two academic citations (Brown et al. 2020, Wei et al. 2022). No other module includes academic citations in its Further Reading section. M08 links to arxiv papers inline in the Further Reading list but without a separate Research Papers subsection.
findings.recommendation: Either add a Research Papers subsection to other modules where relevant (M01 for ReAct, M09 for multi-agent systems research, M08 for meta-prompting papers) or fold M04's citations into the main Further Reading list for consistency.

score_rationale.structural_uniformity: 0.45 — Two incompatible header formats, two Learning Objectives formats, inconsistent heading levels for content sections (## vs ###), Key Concepts section in only 3/12 modules, Do/Don't section in only 5/12 modules, Companion Media in only 5/12 modules. The modules read as if assembled from two or three different authoring passes without a final structural normalization.
score_rationale.terminology_consistency: 0.90 — PRAO, TCEF, GCCF, CLAUDE.md, Streamable HTTP, extended thinking are all used with correct and consistent capitalization and expansion. Minor issue: MCP not expanded on first use in all modules. 'PRAO loop' vs 'PRAO cycle' is a minor variant.
score_rationale.voice_consistency: 0.85 — Consistently second-person, authoritative instructor voice throughout. Slight tonal shift between the more discursive M01-M03 and the more reference-like M04-M08. The later modules (M09-M12) split the difference. No jarring voice breaks.
score_rationale.code_consistency: 0.95 — All code blocks have language identifiers. Code examples are well-formatted and complete. The only gap is the absence of interactive HTML diagram components in M04-M12 that M01-M03 have.
score_rationale.cross_reference_accuracy: 0.85 — All explicit cross-references between modules are accurate. Lab numbering inversion (Module 04 → Lab 05, Module 05 → Lab 04) is confusing but technically documented. Labs 08 and 09 are not referenced by any module.
score_rationale.depth_uniformity: 0.50 — M01-M03 average 7,782 words; M04-M08 average 3,310 words; M09-M12 average 4,443 words. The 2.35x ratio between M01-M03 and M04-M08 is a significant depth disparity, only partly explained by interactive HTML markup.
